_doc: Tess OS Keystone manifest — hand-authored per spec kb/wiki/synthesis/2026-06-27-keystone-architecture.md §2.4. THIS IS THE UPDATER'S WRITE-SCOPE GUARD. Do NOT auto-glob: this repo mixes framework content with clients/, kb/, example-project/, .venv/ and stray root files. tessctl may write ONLY to paths matching owned_globs (refined per-file by tess.lock status). Everything else is unreachable. never_touch is an explicit belt-and-suspenders denylist for user data, secrets, and known-stray junk; precedence: owned_globs WINS over never_touch (so clients/_template/** stays framework-owned even though clients/*/** is denied).

_owned_notes.CLAUDE.md: entry-point doctrine; rendered from CLAUDE.md.tpl + operator/ stubs on update (status core-managed, tier security for its rule blocks).
_owned_notes.conductor/**: the doctrine files; guardrails.md / verification-routing.md / channel-guardrails.md / dispatch-brief.md carry tier:security in tess.lock.
_owned_notes.agents/**: 165 persona prose specs (the agents/ subtree). Framework-owned per spec §2.1 CORE territory and the §2.4 manifest example. NOTE: this was NOT in the caller's parenthetical owned list; added because these are core content, not user data — omitting them would orphan every persona spec from updates. User-authored agents live at .claude/agents/<custom>.md and are protected per-file via tess.lock status:user-created, NOT by excluding this glob.
_owned_notes..claude/agents/**: the 42 compiled dispatch definitions. Per-file status in tess.lock governs actual write behavior: core-managed agents merge; status:user-created agents are never read, never touched.
_owned_notes..claude/commands/**: 26 slash-command definitions (defect #9): core source at .tess/core/commands/**; live at .claude/commands/**. Workflows decision: .claude/workflows/ (3 files — daily-error-report.js, mission-execution.js, clienta-incident.js) are instance-specific operator automation, NOT framework content — they remain user-space and are excluded from core/owned_globs.
_owned_notes..claude/hooks/**: the 6 guard hooks; {{TESS_ROOT}} token substituted on render. LF-enforced (CRLF breaks the shebang).
_owned_notes..claude/settings.json: rendered every update from settings-core.json with {{TESS_ROOT}} substituted (spec Decision 8). Customize via `tessctl publish --tag settings`.
_owned_notes.clients/_template/**: client-folder scaffold — framework-owned (spec §2.4). Distinct from real clients under clients/*/** which are never_touch.

_never_touch_notes.kb/**: internal + client knowledge base (raw/wiki/lint); Tess-maintained user space.
_never_touch_notes.clients/*/**: real client operating systems — data, secrets, repos. clients/_template/** is the sole exception (owned_globs wins).
_never_touch_notes.operator/**: user-authored identity & context, injected at CLAUDE.md render time.
_never_touch_notes..env / .env.* / .claude/settings.local.j: secrets and local allowlists; Claude Code merges settings.local.json natively.
_never_touch_notes.*.local.md / **/*.local.md: append-first shadow files — the 80% customization path (spec §5 Layer 2); appended after core on resolve, never overwritten.
_never_touch_notes..tess/*: tessctl's own transactional state (snapshots, staging, provenance log, lock). NOTE: .tess/core/** is the pristine merge-base source — read by tessctl, never a live-tree write target, so it is intentionally absent from BOTH lists.
_never_touch_notes.python/data layer: main.py, pyproject.toml, uv.lock, .python-version, .venv/** — the repo's Python/uv tooling, not framework doctrine.
_never_touch_notes.example-project/ , starter/ , docs/ , in: non-framework subtrees present in this messy repo; explicitly fenced off.
